component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline, scrollable card within the main column; maintains consistent width and spacing with other policy panels.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Booking policy panel sits directly below the Privacy panel, separated by about 32–48px of vertical whitespace to maintain clear section breaks. It mirrors the same white card treatment: centered within the main column, matching the width and rounded card style of the Privacy panel and navigation card. A bold or semi-bold section heading is left-aligned at the top of the card, followed by well-spaced body text. Any list of booking rules or terms can be presented in stacked paragraphs or bulleted rows, each separated by 8–16px of vertical spacing. The consistent card width, padding, and spacing reinforce a unified document feel as the user scrolls.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline, scrollable card; uses consistent dimensions and spacing relative to other policy panels, creating a visually rhythmic stack.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Shipping policy panel appears next in the vertical sequence, again centered in the main column, with a 32–48px gap above it separating it from the Booking card. Its layout is identical in structure: a white card with soft edges, subtle elevation, and generous inner padding. A strong, left-aligned section title anchors the top, followed by text and any shipping-specific bullet lists or tables, all aligned to the card’s left margin. If there are subsections (e.g., domestic vs. international shipping), they can be broken with extra 16–24px spacing and slightly larger subhead text, but the card still maintains the overall spacious, marketing-style presentation.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline card in the vertical scroll, matching the standard policy panel dimensions and spacing.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Returns & Refunds policy panel follows directly beneath Shipping, separated by 32–48px of white space. As with the other panels, it occupies the full width of the central column within a white, softly elevated card. The section heading is left-aligned at the top; beneath it, key refund conditions and timeframes can be emphasized in short paragraphs or structured lists with 12–16px spacing between items. Any especially important lines (such as deadlines or non-refundable items) can be visually emphasized with slight typographic accent within the card, while still respecting the calm, editorial layout. The card’s height adjusts to fit the content but the strong, centered alignment and generous margins make it feel cohesive with the rest.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline card, continuing the vertical stack and maintaining consistent padding and width within the central column.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Digital Usage policy panel is placed next in order, again with a 32–48px vertical gap above to separate it from the Returns & Refunds section. It is styled as the same central white card, matching width and padding with the other panels. The heading at the top introduces digital content or digital product terms, followed by paragraphs that may include usage rights, licensing, and restrictions. If there are clauses that naturally form bullet lists, they are arranged in a clean, vertical list with 8–12px spacing between lines, preserving the low-to-medium density. The card maintains left-aligned text with ample side padding to keep reading comfortable even when the content is text-heavy.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline, scrollable card; behaves consistently with the other policy sections as part of the vertical reading flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The Gift Card Terms panel is typically the final card in the sequence of detailed policies. It sits below the Digital Usage panel, again separated by 32–48px of whitespace. This card retains the same visual treatment: centered within the main content column, white background, rounded corners, subtle drop shadow, and 32px of padding on all sides. A clear heading (e.g., “Gift Card Terms”) appears at the top, followed by descriptive text and any bullet points outlining how gift cards can be purchased, redeemed, or refunded. The bottom of this card has a bit of extra padding, allowing the content to breathe and providing a soft visual endpoint to the main policy content before any footer or page bottom is reached.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline card at the bottom of the policy stack; essentially the last major block in the primary vertical flow.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The section anchor link control is implemented as a small, unobtrusive yet accessible control associated with each major policy section, and optionally surfaced as part of the floating controls on the right. For each policy panel heading, a subtle link icon appears just to the right of the heading text, aligned on the same baseline; it sits within the card’s content area, inset by about 8px from the heading’s right edge so it does not collide with text. This icon is small and low-contrast until hovered or focused, at which point it becomes clearly visible, inviting users to copy or navigate to a deep link for that section. In addition, on desktop viewports, a compact floating anchor button may appear in the right-hand floating stack, vertically centered relative to the viewport and horizontally positioned just outside the main content column (about 16–24px from the edge of the column), offering a quick way to open a list of section anchors or jump to the top navigation card.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Dual behavior: inline micro-control near each section heading within the cards, plus optionally a small fixed-position button in the right-edge floating cluster that remains visible as the user scrolls.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The contextual cart link is presented as a floating action-style button on the right side of the viewport so it remains accessible without interrupting the reading flow. On desktop, it appears as a rounded rectangular or circular button, roughly 48–56px in diameter or height, with a blue or accent color fill distinct from the red and olive used in headings and bands. It is fixed in position, vertically centered relative to the viewport and horizontally aligned within a narrow column to the right of the main content (roughly 16–24px away from the edge of the main card column, and 24–32px in from the browser’s right edge). This button may be stacked above or below the floating anchor button, with 12–16px vertical spacing between them, forming a small vertical cluster of floating controls. On smaller screens, the cart link can shift down toward the bottom-right corner, maintaining a 16–24px inset from the screen edges so it does not cover essential text.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Fixed-position floating button anchored to the right edge of the viewport; stays visible during scrolling and forms part of a small floating cluster with the anchor control.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The loading state occupies the same central column reserved for the main content, temporarily replacing the navigation card and policy panels while data is being fetched. It appears just below where the main page heading would be (or slightly overlapping that space if the heading is also loading), centered horizontally. The loading indicator could be a medium-sized spinner or a skeleton representation of the nav card, occupying roughly 40–60% of the column’s width, with 24–32px padding around it. Generous whitespace (32–48px) above and below keeps the layout consistent with the page’s airy, editorial feel, and the floating controls on the right remain in place but visually de-emphasized during loading. Once loading completes, this indicator disappears and the navigation card smoothly takes its place.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline, temporary content within the central column; appears centered and scrollable if needed, then is removed from the layout when content is ready.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The error state is displayed in the central column in place of, or directly above, the main content when a loading or retrieval issue occurs. It appears as a compact but noticeable message area—either a simple centered text block or a small alert-style card with a soft red accent border—positioned just below the main page heading. The width aligns with the content column (about 60–70% of the viewport on desktop), and padding of 16–24px around the message ensures it remains readable and visually balanced. There is at least 24–32px of whitespace above and below the error message to match the page’s generous spacing. The floating anchor and cart controls on the right remain visible but are typically functionally disabled or irrelevant until the error is resolved, keeping the user’s focus on the error message and any retry or contact links it contains.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Inline, scrollable message within the main column; appears conditionally and may temporarily replace or precede the normal navigation and content panels.
